page: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a cache hierarchy with 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is supported by a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7a.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier performance across multi-core and memory-intensive operations. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests ranks in the top 10%, supported by strong single-core capabilities. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show top-tier read and write speeds, with cached small-block reads reaching 25488.86 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 652.59 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance also rank in the top 10%, reflecting high resource density and throughput. However, while LLM prompt processing speeds are top-tier across various model sizes, text generation speeds show a notable tradeoff, dropping to average or weak tiers for smaller models.

Given its bare-metal architecture and massive DDR5 memory capacity, the r7a.metal-48xl offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring direct hardware access and substantial memory density without virtualization overhead. The absence of local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached options, which is mitigated by the 50 Gbps network baseline. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-performance computing, intensive software compilation, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 192 dedicated physical cores running at 3.7 GHz, it features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database operation throughput. However, a performance tradeoff is observed in LLM text generation speeds, which range from average to weak despite top-tier prompt processing capabilities. This server is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, and high-performance computing applications requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors with massive DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.

1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for in-memory databases, HPC, and software compilation
